name: Mutable default arguments in add_tag and build_cart accumulate state across calls
description: add_tag uses 'existing_tags: list[str] = []' and build_cart uses 'cart: dict = {}' as default argument values. Python evaluates default arguments once at function-definition time, so the same list/dict object is shared across all calls that omit the argument.

expected_findings.description: add_tag has a mutable list default argument 'existing_tags: list[str] = []'. The list is created once and shared across all calls; appending to it accumulates tags across invocations when the argument is omitted.
build_cart has a mutable dict default argument 'cart: dict = {}'. The same dict object is reused across calls, causing items from previous calls to persist when the argument is omitted.

expected_clean.description: Pure string formatting utilities with no mutable state
